mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, compressing discount rates and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets, increasing corporate earnings visibility and generating positive SPX returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in low-unemployment regimes fall below 0.5% or Rank IC < 0.02 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with declining consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) signal balanced macro conditions, producing positive SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in co-moving HOUST/CPIAUCSL regimes show negative correlation (Rank IC < -0.05) or directional divergence > 40%.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, creating inflationary pressures that drive negative SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO decline periods exceed 1.5% or show positive correlation (Rank IC > 0.05) with supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 310 and FEDFUNDS falls below 5%, the combination signals accommodative policy easing, generating positive SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in triggering regimes show negative correlation (Rank IC < -0.05) or directional divergence > 35%.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) signals economic distress, producing negative SPX returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-moving GDPC1/RSAFS regimes exceed 1.2% or show positive correlation (Rank IC > 0.04) with distress signals.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics, driving positive SPX returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in triggering regimes show negative correlation (Rank IC < -0.06) or directional divergence > 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) above 102.5 combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals mixed supply dynamics, creating negative SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in triggering regimes exceed 0.8% or show positive correlation (Rank IC > 0.04) with mixed supply conditions.
